</imports>

<insights>

- High coupling (0.53) — 16 imports target "prd-tree-operations"
- 18 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Houses both schema primitives (v1.ts, levels.ts, validate.ts) and sync infrastructure (sync-engine.ts, adapter-registry.ts) — consider splitting schema definitions from adapter plumbing if either grows independently
- Integration schemas for Jira and Notion live under store/integration-schemas/ — a clean separation point if additional adapters are added
- Bidirectional imports with unit-core (98 outbound, 16 inbound) suggest some coupling that may be hard to untangle; watch for emergent circular dependencies
- 16 imports flowing from unit-core back into unit-store create a bidirectional dependency loop; this is the primary architectural risk in the rex package and warrants an explicit audit of which core modules depend on store internals.
- Cohesion of 0.47 is borderline — the zone conflates schema definition, canonical transformation, and remote sync adapters into a single community; these could be separated into storage-schema and sync-adapters sub-zones.
- Zone "prd-store-sync" has files across 13 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The 7 reverse imports from unit-store into unit-analyze suggest the store layer has begun pulling in analysis-pipeline logic — if analysis types or helpers have been copied into store modules to break a cycle, that duplication will drift over time
- unit-store → unit-analyze (7 imports) combined with unit-analyze → unit-store (37 imports) forms a second bidirectional loop distinct from the unit-core ↔ unit-store loop; two simultaneous bidirectional cycles in the same zone elevate circular-dependency risk above what either cycle alone would imply
- The zone conflates two distinct change-rate profiles: schema definitions (stable, infrequent changes) and sync adapter infrastructure (volatile, changes with each new integration). Files with different change rates co-located in the same zone inflate merge conflict probability and couple unrelated test suites to unrelated production changes.
- Schema primitives (v1.ts, levels.ts, validate.ts) and remote sync adapters (sync-engine.ts, adapter-registry.ts, integration-schemas/) have mismatched change rates but share a zone. Schema changes should not trigger re-review of adapter code and vice versa — split into a schema-definitions sub-zone and a sync-adapters sub-zone.
- The zone name 'prd-store-sync' emphasizes sync infrastructure but the zone also owns all schema definitions (v1.ts, levels.ts) — the name creates a false impression that the zone is exclusively sync plumbing, which leads new schema-related files to be placed in other zones rather than here.
- 45 files across 13 directories paired with a single-concern name ('store-sync') is a naming-architecture mismatch: the name should either be broadened to 'prd-schema-and-sync' or the zone split to match the implied scope of the current name.
- Rename the zone to 'prd-schema-and-sync' or split it into 'prd-schema' (v1.ts, levels.ts, validate.ts — stable, infrequent changes) and 'prd-sync-adapters' (sync-engine.ts, adapter-registry.ts, integration-schemas/ — volatile). The current name under-describes scope and causes developers to underestimate the zone's breadth.
- [call graph] 899 internal calls, 114 outgoing, 186 incoming (cohesion: 0.89, coupling: 0.11)

</insights>
